channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google rumored to be looking to rent latest Nvidia AI GPU from CoreWeave because it doesn't have enough of them
item.description: Google is rumored to be looking to rent Nvidia Blackwell chips from CoreWeave, because it doesn't have enough.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk, Tesla caught in crosshairs of Trump trade war with China
item.description: President Trump’s escalating trade war with China is putting Elon Musk and Tesla in a tight spot as the electric vehicle manufacturer faces climbing tariffs in one of its most important markets.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: Across Europe and further afield, counterterrorism agencies are grappling with a new generation of young people who are being radicalized online and consuming ultraviolent content produced by extremist groups and their supporters.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: This great free tool brings Pixel-quality image sharpening to any device
item.description: It really is mind-blowing how much incredible stuff we can do with images these days. ’Twasn’t long ago, after all, that advanced image adjustments required pricey desktop-computer software and super-specific knowledge.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from reciprocal tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S. It would also benefit big tech companies like Apple and Samsung.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Razer PC Remote Play lets you stream games to your iPhone – and it could convince me to switch from my gaming PC
item.description: Razer’s PC Remote Play game streaming service lets you play PC games on your iPhone, iPad or Android device.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Trump’s revenge politics has a new target: the cybersecurity official who defied him in 2020
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday ordered federal officials to investigate the former head of the country’s top cybersecurity agency and revoke his security clearances, years after he first disputed Trump’s election fraud claims.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are demanding President Trump confirm that Elon Musk will leave his role in the administration by May 30. A group of 77 lawmakers, led by Rep.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA under review: Trump memo spurs scrutiny and uncertainty
item.description: The Trump administration has issued a directive against Christopher Krebs, the founding director and former head of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) for weaponizing and abusing his government authority during his tenure.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Trump's trade war with China will hit tech prices
item.description: President Trump’s escalating trade war with China is putting a spotlight on the various technology companies and products poised to feel the ripple effects of the ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 10 highest-paying IT skills in 2025 so far
item.description: IT has always been known as a lucrative industry for job seekers, with tech professionals earning an average annual salary of $112,521, according to data from the 2025 Dice Tech Salary Report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google to add on-demand genAI data analyst to Workspace
item.description: Google’s new on-demand generative AI (genAI) analyst for spreadsheets stood out among a slew of new AI features for its Workplace productivity suite announced at this week’s Cloud Next event in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s Agent2Agent open protocol aims to connect disparate agents
item.description: Google has taken the covers off a new open protocol — Agent2Agent (A2A) — that aims to connect agents across disparate ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hydrogen fuel cell trucks hit the roads in Georgia
item.description: Hydrogen trucks debut in Georgia, sparking debates on costs and green freight gains. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson comments on this promising chapter in green logistics evolution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
